description: A designed Department of Labor 'news-headline card' graphic. The image is a photograph of a U.S. Navy amphibious assault ship (an LHD-type vessel with hull number 6 visible on the flight deck) under way at sea, viewed head-on from astern, with several aircraft and tilt-rotor V-22 Osprey craft parked on the flight deck and an American flag flying from the superstructure; a second warship follows behind, with a hazy mountainous coastline on the horizon. Overlaid across the top is the FOX NEWS logo and a large bold black serif headline: 'Labor Dept launches multimillion-dollar shipbuilding training plan as US races to close China gap.' A small embossed agency/White House seal appears in the lower-left corner. The visual style is a clean screenshot-style news card pairing a militarized, patriotic naval image with a real outlet's headline. The propaganda technique is authority-by-association (citing Fox News) and nationalist framing of workforce policy as great-power competition with China. The target audience is the general public and the skilled-trades/maritime workforce, intended to portray the administration as strengthening American industrial and military might.
notable_text: FOX NEWS | Labor Dept launches multimillion-dollar shipbuilding training plan as US races to close China gap
